stories.headline: [NPR] A country-by-country glance at Pope Leo XIV's trip to Africa Pope Leo XIV's four-nation, 11-day trip to Africa is so dizzying in its complexity it recalls some of the globetrotting odysseys of St. John Paul II in his earl
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV is embarking on a four-nation, 11-day trip to Africa, a complex itinerary reminiscent of St. John Paul II's early globetrotting odysseys. The trip is scheduled to take place after the Pope's attendance at the Celebration of the Passion of the Lord in St. Peter's Basilica at the Vatican on Catholic Good Friday, April 3, 2026. The African trip will cover four countries, although the specific nations have not been detailed in the available information. The journey is expected to be significant, given its scope and the Pope's recent activities, including his participation in Vatican events. Further details on the trip's agenda and the countries to be visited are awaited as the trip progresses. The trip's complexity and scale underscore the Pope's commitment to engaging with diverse global communities.

stories.headline: [The Guardian] Nigerian airstrike targeting jihadists reportedly kills at least 100 civilians Officials confirm misfire as Amnesty gives death toll after speaking to survivors of strike on market in Yobe state
stories.summary: A Nigerian airstrike targeting jihadists reportedly killed at least 100 civilians in Yobe state. The strike, aimed at a Boko Haram stronghold, misfired and hit a market in the area. Amnesty International confirmed the death toll after speaking to survivors, while local sources and residents claim the number of casualties may be higher, potentially exceeding 200. The Yobe State Government acknowledged the incident, stating that a military strike was intended to target the jihadist group, but resulted in significant civilian casualties. Officials confirmed a misfire, but did not provide further details. Emergency teams from Yobe State are responding to the incident, with exact figures of casualties still to be confirmed. The airstrike occurred on Saturday in a village near the border with Borno state, a region heavily affected by the jihadist insurgency.

stories.headline: [The Jerusalem Post] IDF kills Hamas terrorists involved in hostage-taking on October 7 Those killed include Ali Sami Mohammad Shakra, a Nukhba unit commander who took part in the kidnapping of Hersh Goldberg-Polin, Alon Ohel, Eliya Cohen, and Or L
stories.summary: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) killed Hamas terrorists involved in hostage-taking on October 7, including Ali Sami Mohammad Shakra, a Nukhba unit commander. Shakra participated in the kidnapping of Hersh Goldberg-Polin, Alon Ohel, Eliya Cohen, and Or Levy. A strike on Saturday targeted a cell of Hamas gunmen, killing a terrorist involved in abducting hostages and two others who held another hostage. Dozens of terrorists in the Gaza Strip were eliminated, including senior operatives and company-level commanders. The IDF also retrieved 59 bodies of hostages who were either killed before being taken to Gaza or died in captivity. Three senior Hamas operatives were killed, who Israel says took part in the October 7 cross-border assault that sparked the ongoing war in Gaza. The IDF has been targeting Hamas terrorists in response to the October 7 attack.

stories.headline: [Yahoo] Iran's Guards will view military vessels approaching strait as ceasefire breach CAIRO, April 12 (Reuters) - Iran's Revolutionary Guards said on Sunday that any military vessels attempting to approach the Strait of Hormuz will be considered
stories.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guards stated that any military vessels approaching the Strait of Hormuz will be considered a breach of ceasefire. The Guards warned that such vessels "will be dealt with harshly and decisively." However, civilian ships are allowed passage through the strait. Iran has permitted a limited number of vessels from friendly countries to transit the area. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps emphasized that the Strait of Hormuz is open to civilian vessels, but armed vessels will face a severe response. This announcement comes after Iran denied that any American military vessels had transited the Strait of Hormuz, amid heightened tensions in the region. The Guards' statement aims to assert control over the strategic waterway, which is crucial for international oil trade.

stories.headline: France Plans Shift from Windows to Linux in Push for Digital Sovereignty The French government is moving to transition all state workstations from Microsoft Windows to Linux, in a strategic shift aimed
stories.summary: The French government is transitioning 2.5 million civil servant workstations from Microsoft Windows to Linux, aiming to reduce dependence on American software. Led by the Interministerial Digital Directorate (DINUM), the move is part of France's digital sovereignty plans. The country seeks to decrease its reliance on US technology, citing digital sovereignty, cost savings, and security as motivations. The multi-year initiative is Europe's most ambitious open-source migration effort to date. France has formally launched a government-wide plan to replace Microsoft Windows with Linux on civil service desktops. This shift is the latest step in France's efforts to strengthen its digital sovereignty by adopting locally developed software and cloud services, reducing reliance on US tech giants. The move marks a significant rejection of US tech dominance by a major Western democracy.

stories.headline: Rockstar Games says hack will have ‘no impact’ Player data isn’t suspected to be compromised.
stories.summary: Rockstar Games has confirmed a third-party data breach, but claims it will have "no impact" on the company or its players. A limited amount of non-material company information was accessed, according to a spokesperson. The hack appears to have targeted corporate data, such as financial records, marketing data, or contracts, rather than player information. The company has stated that the breach does not compromise player data. The hacker group has made claims, but Rockstar Games maintains that the incident is minor and will not affect its operations or customers. The spokesperson has reassured multiple outlets that the breach is contained and will not have any significant consequences for the company or its players. The exact nature of the compromised data is unclear, but Rockstar Games is downplaying the incident.

stories.headline: Huawei unveils entry-level smartphone with Kirin 8000 processor Huawei has launched another new sibling in its existing Enjoy 90 series, namely, the Huawei Enjoy 90m Plus.
stories.summary: Huawei has launched the Enjoy 90m Plus, an entry-level smartphone in its Enjoy 90 series. The device features a Kirin 8000 processor, WiFi 7, a 120 Hz LCD display, and a large 6,620 mAh battery with 40 W wired charging support. It runs on HarmonyOS 6.0, offering efficient mid-range mobile performance and security features like an AI anti-fraud system. The phone's configuration is similar to the Enjoy 90 Plus, but it comes in an exclusive coral orange color scheme and has a different lens deco design. The price of the Huawei Enjoy 90m Plus has not been announced yet. The phone is now listed on Huawei's official website, joining the existing Enjoy 90 series, which includes the Enjoy 90, Enjoy 90 Plus, and Enjoy 90 Pro Max.

stories.headline: As WhatsApp faces lawsuit, Elon Musk launches 'no tracking' messaging app XChat App will offer encrypted chats, voice and video calls, large group messaging and disappearing messages, initially for iPhone and iPad users only, with no ads or
stories.summary: Elon Musk's social media platform X is launching a standalone messaging app called XChat on April 17, 2026. The app will offer encrypted chats, voice and video calls, large group messaging, and disappearing messages. Initially, it will be available for iPhone and iPad users only, with no ads or phone number required to sign up. XChat features end-to-end encryption, no data tracking, and the ability to block attempts to screenshot conversations. The app's launch comes as WhatsApp faces a lawsuit, and XChat's pitch is to provide a private and secure messaging experience. The app is currently available for pre-order on the App Store, with a full launch expected next Thursday. XChat's features and approach are similar to those of Signal, but with a broader super-app vision. The app's focus on privacy and security may appeal to users concerned about data tracking.

stories.headline: Chelsea 0-3 Manchester City, Premier League: Post-match reaction, ratings Another day, another collapse
stories.summary: Manchester City defeated Chelsea 3-0 at Stamford Bridge in a Premier League match. The win marked City's fourth three-goal defeat of Chelsea in their last six games. Goals from Rico Lewis, Julián Álvarez, and Phil Foden secured the victory for Manchester City. The loss leaves Chelsea in sixth place, four points behind fifth and only one point ahead of seventh and eighth. Manchester City's win cuts the gap with league leaders Arsenal to six points. The match saw City dominate the second half, with their goals coming after a closely contested first half. The result tightens the Premier League table, with only three points separating sixth from eleventh place. Manchester City's win completes a successful weekend for the team, solidifying their position in the league.

stories.headline: Phil Garner, a three-time All-Star infielder who went on to manage the Astros to their first World Series appearance, has died. He was 76.
stories.summary: Phil Garner, a three-time All-Star infielder, has died at the age of 76. He played 16 seasons in Major League Baseball, spending time with the Athletics and Pirates. After his playing career, Garner went on to manage the Houston Astros, leading them to their first World Series appearance. Garner's family announced his passing on Sunday, stating that he died on Saturday after a two-plus-year battle with pancreatic cancer. Garner's managerial career with the Astros spanned seven years, culminating in the team's first World Series appearance. He was a respected figure in the baseball community, known for his achievements as both a player and a manager. Garner's legacy will be remembered by fans and colleagues alike, honoring his contributions to the sport. His death marks the loss of a notable figure in baseball history.

stories.headline: Lille ease back into third in Ligue 1 with Toulouse win Lille reclaimed third place in Ligue 1 with a comfortable 4-0 win away to 10-man Toulouse on Sunday.
stories.summary: Lille reclaimed third place in Ligue 1 with a 4-0 win away to 10-man Toulouse on Sunday. The victory allowed Lille to move back into third position in the league standings. Toulouse, who are in 10th place, played with 10 men for part of the match. Meanwhile, Lyon remained in contention for direct Champions League qualification with a 2-0 home win over mid-table Lorient later on Sunday. The win keeps Lyon in fifth place, still competing for a spot in the Champions League. Lille's win over Toulouse was a significant one, as it helped them regain their position in the top three of the Ligue 1 table. The match was a comfortable win for Lille, with Toulouse unable to mount a significant challenge.

stories.headline: Bordeaux v Toulouse: Five takeaways from a 30-15 victory for Bordeaux-Begles over Toulouse in the Investec Champions Cup quarter-final
stories.summary: Bordeaux-Begles defeated Toulouse 30-15 in the Investec Champions Cup quarter-final. The match took place at Stade Chaban-Delmas on April 12, 2026, with a 15:00 UK kick-off time, and was broadcast on Premier Sports. This victory marks a significant win for Bordeaux-Begles, etching their name into European rugby folklore. The result is reminiscent of Brive's 1998 European Cup triumph over Toulouse. Bordeaux-Begles' performance is considered a statement win in a major European competition, with the team delivering a strong showing against a tough opponent. The 30-15 scoreline reflects Bordeaux-Begles' dominance in the match, securing their place in the next round of the tournament.

stories.headline: Man with ₹10 crore savings explains why he wants to leave India: 'My loyalty to India has hit its limit'
stories.summary: A man in his 50s with ₹10 crore in assets has expressed his desire to leave India, citing the daily struggles of living in cities like Bengaluru or Delhi. Despite his significant savings, he feels that day-to-day life is a "daily battle". His decision is not driven by a dislike for the country, but rather a desire for a easier and more predictable life abroad. This sentiment is not unique, as some Indians who have lived abroad have noted the differences in quality of life. In contrast, an Indian professional who earned ₹2.9 crore annually in the US has decided to return to India, citing family connections, emotional well-being, and cultural roots as key reasons. The man's post has sparked a discussion about the pros and cons of living in India versus abroad.

stories.headline: Countdown for new Bihar govt begins with last cabinet meet on April 14
stories.summary: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar will preside over his last cabinet meeting on April 14, scheduled to begin at 11 am. After the meeting, Kumar will tender his resignation, marking the beginning of the countdown for the formation of a new government in the state. A new government, with a first-ever chief minister, is expected to be sworn in on Wednesday. Kumar's resignation and the swearing-in of the new government will bring an end to his current term. The outgoing chief minister has also started shifting his residence to 7, Circular Road, as preparations for the transition are underway. The cabinet meeting on April 14 will be the last under Kumar's leadership before he steps down and a new government takes over in Bihar.

stories.headline: Why Is Vijay Performing Such Risky Stunts During the Campaign?
stories.summary: Vijay, founder of the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), has been performing risky stunts during his campaign rallies, sparking controversy and concerns over crowd safety. Despite a tragic incident on September 27, 2025, where 41 people were killed and 100 injured in a crowd crush at a rally in Karur district, Tamil Nadu, Vijay continues to engage in volatile activities. His rallies have drawn massive crowds, but also raised concerns over chaos, risky fan behavior, and aggressive security actions. The Chennai police have registered a case against Vijay for allegedly violating election conduct rules, while the party accuses authorities of failing to provide adequate security. The incidents have led to widespread criticism, with many calling Vijay's actions irresponsible and potentially disastrous. The TVK leader's campaign style has become a subject of debate ahead of the 2026 elections.

stories.headline: Ap Intermediate 1st 2nd Year Results Date, Time, Link at bie.ap.gov.in
stories.summary: The Board of Intermediate Education, Andhra Pradesh (BIEAP) is expected to release the AP Intermediate 1st and 2nd year results 2026 soon. Over 10.57 lakh students will receive their marks memos, which can be downloaded via bie.ap.gov.in. Students are advised to keep their hall tickets ready. The results will be available on the official websites - bie.ap.gov.in and resultsbie.ap.gov.in. A confirmed date and time for the announcement of the results will be available on the website. The results are expected to be declared this week, with students able to check their marks memos online once released. The BIEAP will announce the exact date and time for the results declaration, which will be available on the official website.

stories.headline: Wall Street trading desks poised for $40B quarter amid geopolitical turmoil
stories.summary: Major US banks are expected to report over $40 billion in combined Q1 trading revenue, their strongest performance in more than a decade. This surge is driven by geopolitical turmoil, including the US's tensions with Iran and trade war chaos, which has led to increased market volatility. Wall Street's biggest banks, such as Bank of America and Goldman Sachs, are set to unveil a record $18 billion stock-trading haul for the first quarter. As investors reposition their portfolios, trading desks are booming, with banks cashing in on the uncertainty. The strong trading revenue is expected to be a highlight of the banks' Q1 earnings reports, with investors being advised to focus on quality and high cash flow assets amidst the market turmoil. This performance would mark a significant increase in trading revenue for the banks.

stories.headline: Value in Volatility: Why smart money hunts ‘gaps’ in uncertain markets
stories.summary: Global market volatility, driven by geopolitics, inflation, and interest rates, is reviving value investing principles. Veteran investor Arnold Van Den Berg notes that uncertainty creates mispriced assets, offering opportunities for disciplined investors focused on intrinsic value. Smart money is hunting for "gaps" in uncertain markets, where assets are undervalued due to market noise and sentiment-driven swings. This approach allows investors to capitalize on long-term opportunities amidst short-term turmoil. A thoughtful, diversified portfolio can provide stability and cushion the blow when one sector underperforms, while positioning for gains when another outperforms. Disciplined investors are using strategies like dollar-cost averaging to reduce risk and stress, and are being rewarded for their patience and savvy in a market where timing is increasingly difficult. This approach is making smart money look smart again.

stories.headline: Global market volatility driven by geopolitics, inflation, and rates is reviving value investing principles
stories.summary: Global market volatility is being driven by geopolitics, inflation, and interest rates, leading to a revival of value investing principles. Veteran investor Arnold Van Den Berg notes that uncertainty creates mispriced assets, offering opportunities. Key drivers of market volatility include Chinese economic stimulus measures and geopolitical developments, such as the US-Iran conflict. The current financial landscape is marked by high uncertainty, with technological disruption and geopolitical tensions contributing to market instability. As a result, value-based investing principles are gaining relevance, as investors seek to capitalize on mispriced assets. Investing in such conditions involves risk, including potential losses, and past performance is not a guarantee of future results. The ongoing geopolitical tensions and economic shifts are expected to continue driving market volatility in the coming months.

stories.headline: Major US banks may top $40B in Q1 trading revenue as geopolitics drive volatility
stories.summary: Major US banks, including JPMorgan Chase and Goldman Sachs, are expected to post over $40 billion in trading revenue for the first quarter. Geopolitical tensions have driven sharp movements across financial markets, resulting in increased trading activity. This surge in revenue is attributed to the heightened volatility, which has led to a rise in trading volumes. The combined trading revenues of Wall Street's largest banks are anticipated to exceed $40 billion for the quarter. Historically, periods of high trading revenue have often preceded broader economic recovery, as companies use public funds to reinvest in research and development, and expansion. The strong performance is expected to be reflected in the banks' upcoming earnings reports.

stories.headline: Modi Accuses TMC of Appeasement Politics, Banerjee Alleges BJP's Delimitation Plot
stories.summary: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi accused the Trinamool Congress (TMC) of appeasement politics, alleging it has contributed to demographic changes and hindered development in north Bengal. He claimed central welfare schemes had been stalled and urged voters to back a "double-engine government" to accelerate growth. In response, West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee alleged that the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) plans to divide the state through delimitation, potentially merging districts with neighboring states like Bihar or Odisha. Banerjee also accused the BJP of vote manipulation and fear politics over the National Register of Citizens (NRC). The exchange comes ahead of polls, with the Uniform Civil Code being a recent point of contention between the TMC and BJP in Bengal. Modi criticized the TMC's allocation of Rs 6,000 crore for Madrassas development, comparing it to the insufficient funds for north Bengal.

stories.headline: TMC supports 'tukde-tukde gang' to cut Siliguri Corridor: PM Modi in Bengal rally
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi accused the Trinamool Congress (TMC) of supporting the "tukde-tukde gang" who threatened to cut off the Northeast from the rest of India by snapping the strategic Siliguri Corridor. Modi alleged that the TMC provided support to this group from the streets to Parliament due to its "appeasement politics". The Siliguri Corridor is a vital connection between the Northeast and the rest of the country. Modi made these comments during a rally in poll-bound West Bengal, stating that the TMC's actions were a threat to national integrity. He claimed that the TMC took the "tukde-tukde gang" from the streets to Parliament, implying that the party backed anti-national elements. The Prime Minister's remarks were part of his campaign in West Bengal, where elections are scheduled to take place.

stories.headline: No other headlines provided to select from, therefore only two can be returned.
stories.summary: The Washington Post, Al Jazeera, The New York Times, NPR, and Reuters provide live coverage and the latest headlines on US and international news. These news outlets offer a range of content, including breaking news, investigations, opinion pieces, photos, and videos. The New York Times has journalists in over 150 countries, providing global coverage. NPR offers news, audio, and podcasts on various topics, including politics, business, science, and technology. Reuters provides breaking international news coverage, making it a go-to source for global news. These outlets allow readers to stay up-to-date on the latest developments and major events from around the world, with fact-based reporting and exclusive content. Their online platforms offer a wealth of information, enabling users to access news from every corner of the globe.

stories.headline: No other headlines provided to select from, therefore only two can be returned.
stories.summary: The Washington Post and other news sources, including NPR and Al Jazeera, provide live coverage and the latest headlines on US and international news, politics, business, and technology. These sources offer in-depth coverage of breaking stories, national and world events, and extended coverage of major events. Al Jazeera provides fact-based news, exclusive video footage, and updated maps to keep readers informed. For those with limited time, "ET in 2 Minutes" offers a concise snapshot of important events in India and the world. This format is designed to provide a quick overview of current events, with research showing that an average person can read 400-500 words in two minutes, making it an efficient way to stay informed about global news and developments.

stories.headline: Stock market outlook: US-Iran talks collapse, oil prices to weigh on sentiment; inflation data, Q4 earnings in focus
stories.summary: The collapse of US-Iran talks in Pakistan is expected to weigh on investor sentiment when markets open on Monday. Analysts say crude oil prices will also dictate market trends in a holiday-shortened week. The talks ended without a deal, and this development, along with key macroeconomic data, will drive trends. US inflation data surged as expected, and the market will also track the US Producer Price Index (PPI) and speeches from Federal officials. Stock markets will remain closed on Tuesday due to Baba Saheb Ambedkar Jayanti. Precious metals like gold and silver are expected to remain volatile due to fragile sentiment. Investors will closely monitor domestic inflation data and global cues, including crude oil prices, to gauge market direction. The breakdown of US-Iran negotiations will keep sentiment fragile, influencing market trends in the week ahead.

stories.headline: US-Iran Standoff: What Does This Mean For Indian Stock Market Amid Q4 Momentum?
stories.summary: The US-Iran standoff continues with no agreement reached in ceasefire discussions held in Pakistan on Sunday. The conflict has disrupted the Middle East for over two months, with both sides failing to provide clarity on movement via the Strait of Hormuz. This has raised concerns for the Indian stock market, which is currently experiencing Q4 momentum. The market outlook for the next week will be influenced by US-Iran talks, Q4 earnings, and oil prices. The White House has suggested that US President Donald Trump may decide on Iran attacks within the next two weeks, amid a substantial chance of negotiations. The ongoing tensions are expected to impact stock investors, who are already on high alert due to the broadening of the Iran-Israel conflict. Oil prices and geopolitical developments will be closely watched.

stories.headline: Stock Market Outlook For Next Week Looks Positive But Cautious, Nifty At 24,050 And Sensex At 77,550 With Oil, Earnings & US-Iran Talks In
stories.summary: The Indian stock market is expected to remain positive but cautious next week, with the Nifty at 24,050 and Sensex at 77,550. The market posted robust gains on Friday, driven by positive global cues and hopes of a temporary US-Iran ceasefire. However, ongoing geopolitical uncertainties limited the momentum of gains. Key triggers for the market next week include Q4 earnings, US-Iran talks, and crude oil prices. The Nifty and Sensex gained nearly 6% after six weeks of losses, with the Nifty settling at 24,050.60 and the Sensex ending at 77,550.25. Analysts expect cautious trading ahead, driven by global geopolitical tensions and currency and crude oil movements. The market's outlook is influenced by steady domestic fundamentals and positive global developments. Investors will closely watch the upcoming Q4 results and developments in US-Iran talks for market direction.

stories.headline: Inconclusive US-Iran talks, oil prices, inflation data to dictate investors' sentiment: Analysts
stories.summary: US-Iran talks in Pakistan ended without a deal, which is expected to impact investors' sentiment when markets open on Monday. Analysts say the inconclusive talks, along with rising crude oil prices and inflation data, will dictate market trends in a holiday-shortened week. The developments in West Asia and oil prices will be closely watched, as they are likely to drive market volatility and affect the Indian Rupee exchange rate. The US inflation data surged as expected, due to a recent jump, adding to the factors influencing investor sentiment. With equities mixed on Friday and oil prices dipping, investors are anxiously awaiting the outcome of the US-Iran talks and the impact of inflation data on the market. The combination of these factors is expected to lead to market volatility in the coming week.
